data.title: Kodak outlines post-pension reversion growth as 2025 operational EBITDA rises 144%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Eastman Kodak Company (KODK) Q4 2025

Management View

  • James Continenza, Executive Chairman & CEO, stated the company finished the quarter and year “very strong,” highlighting a “tale of 2 halves” with notable improvements in the third and fourth quarters. He attributed the difference to the company’s long-term investments now “starting to pay off.”
  • Continenza emphasized that “our balance sheet hasn't been this strong in many years,” referencing debt reduction and over $40 million of interest expense reduction. He signaled the company is “poised for growth,” with “heavily delevered, streamlined operations and investments in new products.”
  • The CEO shared that Kodak took out over $200 million in operating expense over the last few years, invested heavily in new infrastructure and products, and is “well positioned to drive growth.” He noted Q4 revenues of $290 million, up 9%, with both AM&C and Print contributing to growth.
  • On product performance, Continenza reported AM&C revenue was up 25% for the fourth quarter, citing the launch of a direct distribution brand of still films. The commercial print division’s PROSPER 520 moved from controlled introduction to full production. Investments in AI, machine learning, and a rapid response service system were also referenced.
  • Brand licensing was highlighted as a continued “significant contributor” to gross profit and awareness, particularly in Asia.
  • David Bullwinkle, CFO & Senior VP, stated: “This transaction generated approximately $1.023 billion in pension reversion proceeds, a combination of cash and investment assets that strengthens our balance sheet, establishes an overfunding of the new Kodak cash balance pension plan, reduces our ongoing interest expense and supports future growth.”
  • Bullwinkle outlined that the company ended 2025 with a cash balance of $337 million and detailed amendments to the Series B preferred equity obligation, including an extension to June 2029 and a cumulative dividend rate change to 6% per annum.

Outlook

  • Continenza reiterated Kodak is “focused on growth following a very strong 2025,” with a strong balance sheet and “promising new investments in our pharma division, in our battery coating.” The CEO stressed, “We are pleased with the direction we're in. We have a long way to go. But I can tell you, this is a good start, especially having the balance sheet out of the way.”
  • Bullwinkle confirmed that “Kodak is in a net positive cash position relative to our $300 million in term loan and Series B preferred equity obligations with a cash balance of $337 million as of December 31, 2025.”

Financial Results

  • Kodak reported fourth quarter revenue of $290 million, a $24 million or 9% increase year-over-year. Gross profit was $67 million, up $16 million or 31% from 2024. Gross profit percentage rose to 23% from 19%.
  • The company recorded a GAAP net loss of $108 million for Q4, compared with net income of $26 million in the prior year; primary drivers were a $153 million excise tax expense and a $7 million loss on early extinguishment of debt, partially offset by a $66 million gain on the settlement of the KRIP plan.
  • Adjusted for nonrecurring items, net loss was $12 million for Q4, compared to net income of $27 million in the prior year.
  • Operational EBITDA for Q4 was $22 million, up $13 million or 144% year-over-year, driven by improved pricing and higher volume, partially offset by higher manufacturing costs.
  • Kodak ended the quarter with $337 million in unrestricted cash. For the full year, operational EBITDA was $62 million, an increase of $36 million or 138% year-over-year.
  • The company reduced the principal balance of term loans by $303 million, ending at $200 million.

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE. No formal Q&A session was held during the call, as explicitly stated by management. Follow-up is available through the Investor Relations team.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ sentiment is not directly observable due to the lack of a Q&A session, but the structure of the call suggests a neutral to slightly positive environment, as management controlled the narrative.
  • Management’s sentiment in prepared remarks was confident and optimistic, with Continenza stating, “Kodak today is on a very solid foundation for growth. We have a strong balance sheet with more cash than debt.” Bullwinkle’s tone was factual and highlighted deliberate actions to “enhance the company’s long-term stability.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a confident tone but provided more detail on balance sheet improvements and future growth orientation. Analyst sentiment cannot be compared due to the absence of questions in both quarters.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 guidance language shifted to a more explicit growth focus, with management emphasizing streamlining, deleveraging, and operational improvements post-pension reversion.
  • Strategic focus evolved from pension reversion execution in Q3 to growth and leveraging a strengthened balance sheet in Q4.
  • Key metrics showed increased revenue ($290 million in Q4 vs. $269 million in Q3), higher operational EBITDA, and greater unrestricted cash.
  • Management’s confidence grew, with less discussion of going concern and more on forward growth, while analysts’ tone remains unassessable due to no Q&A in either quarter.
  • Strategic priorities shifted from deleveraging and pension settlement in Q3 to growth initiatives, new product launches, and market expansion in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Bullwinkle acknowledged a “challenging global environment marked by economic and geopolitical uncertainty, including pressures on global trade and inflation.”
  • The company incurred a $153 million excise tax expense related to the KRIP reversion surplus and a $7 million loss on early extinguishment of debt.
  • Management addressed ongoing restructuring costs and a reduction in noncash pension income as weighing on year-over-year comparisons.
  • Mitigation strategies included balance sheet strengthening, extension of debt maturities, continued cost controls, and operational streamlining.

Final Takeaway

Kodak’s management emphasized the company’s successful execution on long-term investment and balance sheet strategies, resulting in strengthened liquidity, significant debt reduction, and improved operational performance. With core businesses and new investments contributing to growth, and a net positive cash position for the first time in years, Kodak now aims to focus on sustainable growth through product innovation and expansion, supported by a streamlined organization and continued operational discipline.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Identiv outlines 26%–35% Q1 2026 sales growth target through BLE smart label expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Identiv (INVE) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Kirsten Newquist highlighted "significant advancements in the development of the specialized Bluetooth Low Energy, BLE, smart label in collaboration with IFCO," and announced a multiyear agreement to serve as "exclusive supplier for committed manufacturing volumes." She described this as a "major milestone in our high-growth BLE strategy and reinforces Identiv's leadership in scalable BLE-enabled solutions for complex global industries."
  • Newquist stated that "fourth quarter sales of $6.2 million exceeded our guidance with all other key financial metrics also coming in ahead of expectations." She attributed gross profit margin strength to the "successful completion of our 2-year transition of production from Singapore to our new state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Thailand," emphasizing reduced costs and increased efficiency.
  • CFO Edward Kirnbauer reported, "In the fourth quarter of 2025, we delivered $6.2 million in revenue, which exceeded our previously announced guidance range compared to $6.7 million in Q4 2024." Kirnbauer also cited "GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins were 18.1% and 25.6%, respectively, compared to GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins of negative 14.9% and negative 5.2%, respectively, in Q4 2024."
  • Kirnbauer noted, "We exited Q4 2025 with $128.9 million in cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash, which is a sequential increase of $2.3 million over the third quarter of 2025."

Outlook

  • The company anticipates Q1 2026 sales of "$6.7 million to $7.2 million, which includes the benefit of one of our new customers ordering their full year volume in Q1. This would be an anticipated increase of 26% to 35% over the $5.3 million in sales that we reported for Q1 of 2025," according to CFO Kirnbauer.
  • Management expects "near-term variability in gross margins as we begin scaling production for the IFCO program and for another new customer in Q1." Newquist stated, "As these programs mature and volume scale, we believe they will support attractive long-term margin performance."
  • Cash usage for 2026 is projected at "$14 million to $16 million, excluding strategic review-related costs," with $3.5 million in capital expenditures, $1 million for working capital, and $1.5 million to purchase chips for long-term orders.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2025 revenue was $6.2 million, up from Q3’s $5.0 million but down from Q4 2024’s $6.7 million, as the company exited lower-margin business.
  • GAAP net loss from continuing operations was $3.7 million or $0.16 per share, compared to a $4.3 million loss or $0.19 per share in Q4 2024.
  • Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA loss for Q4 2025 was $2.5 million compared to $4.5 million in Q4 2024.
  • Fiscal year 2025 revenue was $21.5 million, a decrease of $5.1 million year-over-year, while GAAP net loss was $18 million, improved from $25.9 million in 2024.
  • Gross margin improvements were linked to the move to the Thailand facility, with Q4 2025 non-GAAP gross margin at 25.6%, versus negative 5.2% in the prior year.

Q&A

  • Jaeson Schmidt, Lake Street: Asked about the IFCO revenue opportunity and margin profile. Newquist responded, "We are scaling up to 100 million units of capacity per year...gross margin will be less than our target gross margin of 30%, but still a very, very great opportunity for us."
  • Schmidt: Queried supplier exclusivity. Newquist answered, "It's an exclusive agreement. We will be developing this product exclusively for them, and then we will be the exclusive supplier for them over the term of the agreement."
  • Schmidt: Asked about pipeline breakdown. Newquist shared, "It's roughly 25% of them are for health care...25% for logistics, probably another 25% for food and beverage and then the rest is a variety of applications."
  • Rian Bisson, Craig-Hallum: Inquired about pipeline margin targets and December quarter revenue from new opportunities. Newquist clarified, "There would be nothing in our quarter 4 that is in our NPD pipeline...I would still say that roughly 2/3 of the opportunities in the NPD pipeline would be in higher margin targets."
  • Bisson: Asked about the IFCO development phase. Newquist confirmed, "We are still making final design changes...the significant ramp-up will be at the end of the year, quarter 4."
  • Unknown Analyst, B. Riley: Sought details on 2026 gross margin drivers. Kirnbauer replied, "We did finish the year at a non-GAAP 25.6% margin. But as we move into 2026, we do anticipate near-term variability...we expect expansion of the margin as we progress through 2026 with our current customer base."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for specifics on margins, revenue pipeline composition, and IFCO ramp timing, reflecting a slightly positive but cautious outlook as they probed for clarity on profitability and execution.
  • Management maintained a positive and confident tone, frequently referencing "encouraging momentum," "solid step forward," and a "clear mission," while also cautioning about near-term margin variability and emphasizing long-term prospects.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted from steady progress and operational transition to highlighting substantial margin improvements, exclusive supply agreements, and accelerating growth expectations.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 guidance improved from Q3’s $5.4–$5.9 million range to actual Q4 sales of $6.2 million, with Q1 2026 guidance forecasting a significant year-over-year increase.
  • Gross margin expanded sharply in Q4, bolstered by the full shift to Thailand production, compared to gradual improvements discussed in Q3.
  • Strategic focus moved from completing operational transitions and building the pipeline in Q3 to executing exclusive agreements and preparing for major production scale-ups in Q4.
  • Analysts shifted from focusing on operational transitions and pipeline conversion rates in Q3 to probing the specifics of large-scale customer agreements and the breakdown of new business opportunities in Q4.
  • Management confidence increased, evident in direct references to exceeding targets and exclusive agreements, compared to Q3’s emphasis on foundational progress.

Risks and Concerns

  • Near-term margin variability is anticipated during the scale-up of IFCO and onboarding of new customers.
  • The dependency on achieving final development milestones for mass production with IFCO may delay full-scale revenue realization.
  • Strategic review-related costs and capital investment requirements remain a factor for 2026 cash usage.
  • Management’s mitigation includes focusing on operational discipline, predictive demand planning, and targeted cost reduction initiatives.

Final Takeaway

Management underscored the transition to a high-value IoT pure-play, highlighted by the exclusive IFCO BLE smart label agreement, robust conversion of pipeline opportunities, and substantial operational cost improvements from the Thailand facility. With Q4 financials ahead of guidance and targets set for 26%–35% Q1 sales growth, the company signaled readiness to scale production, deepen customer engagement, and accelerate growth across BLE and IoT segments in 2026, while remaining mindful of gross margin variability during new program ramps.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Health Catalyst refrains from 2026 annual guidance as new CEO launches operational overhaul and strategic review
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Health Catalyst (HCAT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Benjamin Albert opened the call by addressing his recent appointment, stating, "I stepped into the CEO role last month following Dan Burton's departure as CEO and from the Board of Directors." He emphasized a "renewed sense of focus and execution," highlighting a comprehensive business review and swift moves to "tighten leadership focus and execution discipline." New general managers were appointed for interoperability and cybersecurity, and the Chief Commercial Officer role transitioned to an internal successor. Searches for a Chief Operating Officer and Chief Marketing Officer are underway to "strengthen operational rigor and to clarify and elevate our position within the market."
  • Albert described actions to review the company's cost structure, expand technology bookings and margins, and drive cash flow generation. He stressed a "back-to-basics approach" and a sharper, simplified commercial story, stating, "We will strengthen and simplify our commercial engine to drive technology ARR bookings. We will improve retention through more predictable migrations and clear client value realization."
  • The CEO announced a shift in reporting, saying, "We will focus on providing a new set of bookings and retention metrics that are easier to understand, align directly with our execution and clearly reflect how we operate the business."
  • Albert concluded that, "Given this work and the significant impact some of it may have on our financial results going forward, we are not yet in a position to provide annual guidance. Today, we are sharing first quarter revenue and adjusted EBITDA guidance only."
  • CFO Jason Alger stated, "For the full year of 2025, we generated $311.1 million in revenue and $41.4 million of adjusted EBITDA." He reported 32 net new logos, an average ARR plus nonrecurring revenue near the midpoint of the $300,000 to $700,000 range, and a tech plus TAMs dollar-based retention rate of 93%. Alger commented, "Adjusted gross margin for the fourth quarter was 53.5% compared to 46.6% in the prior year period."

Outlook

  • Management is not providing 2026 annual guidance at this time. Albert explained, "We plan to come back to the market with our full year revenue and adjusted EBITDA guidance no later than our first quarter earnings call in May."
  • For Q1 2026, Alger guided to "total revenue of $68 million to $70 million and adjusted EBITDA of $7 million to $8 million." The expected sequential revenue decline is attributed to reductions in TEMS-related revenue (about $2 million), continued migration pressure from DOS to Ignite (about $1.5 million), and a decrease in nonrecurring revenue (about $1.5 million).
  • Alger highlighted that "across 2026 and 2027, we've been notified of roughly $12.5 million in DOS-related ARR downsell and churn," and that $52 million in DOS-related ARR is subject to negotiation during this period.

Financial Results

  • For Q4 2025, total revenue was $74.7 million. Technology revenue reached $51.9 million, while professional services revenue was $22.8 million.
  • Alger noted, "Adjusted operating expenses were $26.2 million, representing 35% of revenue compared to $29.2 million or 37% of revenue in the fourth quarter of 2024."
  • For the full year, adjusted gross margin was 51.1%, with technology gross margin at 67.4% and professional services gross margin at 18.3%.
  • Impairment charges on goodwill and intangible assets of $110.2 million were reported, primarily due to a decrease in market cap and forecast revisions, driving GAAP net loss from $69.5 million in 2024 to $178 million in 2025.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2025 was $13.8 million. Adjusted net income per share in Q4 and full year 2025 was $0.08 and $0.19, respectively.
  • The company ended the year with approximately $96 million of cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments, and $161 million of term loan debt outstanding.

Q&A

  • Stanislav Berenshteyn, Wells Fargo: Asked if the strategic review includes a potential company sale. CEO Albert answered, "We are really doing this assessment to best position ourselves for success and align to create shareholder value," but did not confirm or deny a sale.
  • Richard Close, Canaccord Genuity: Requested details on the $52 million DOS-related ARR at risk. CFO Alger clarified, "It's the data platform infrastructure where we're seeing the highest degree of pressure related to this migration... that's where we have $35 million of data platform infrastructure ARR that we're working with our clients on plans to retain."
  • Jeffrey Garro, Stephens: Inquired about Q4 bookings and demand environment. CEO Albert noted, "The market is in great need of the ability to better manage their costs to drive clinical quality and to engage and attract new consumers... profitability pockets are eroding for our provider clients."
  • Elizabeth Anderson, Evercore: Asked where Health Catalyst has a "right to win." Albert replied, "We have 15 years in this industry, we've done thousands of projects. We have tremendous content and intellectual property to enable our AI to help guide our clients through change management."
  • Jenny Shen, BTIG: Queried about the split between roll-offs and down sells. CFO Alger responded, "We don't generally lose enterprise relationships... where we are seeing the pressure... is on the data platform infrastructure side."
  • Jessica Tassan, Piper Sandler: Asked for specifics on applications and platform disintermediation. CEO Albert detailed applications like "clinical cost intelligence" and "power labor" and emphasized efforts to prevent commoditization through expertise and integration.
  • Sarah James, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about margin durability and levers for 2026. CFO Alger cited "pressure associated with the DOS to Ignite migration... duplicate hosting costs," and said migration-related costs are expected to be removed after completion.
  • Daniel Grosslight, Citi: Sought a breakdown of $65 million ARR at risk. CFO Alger explained the timing, stating, "We expect about 75% of that to impact 2026... more of that will come on probably around midyear and going into the later half of 2026."
  • Richard Close, Canaccord: Asked about performance of acquired apps. CEO Albert said, "Most of our applications align to those areas, and we see opportunities across."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on the strategic review, sale prospects, migration headwinds, and ARR at risk, reflecting a slightly negative to neutral tone with repeated questions about downside risk and the durability of margins. Questions about the possibility of a sale and recurring revenue loss indicate clear concern.
  • Management's prepared remarks were confident and focused on operational discipline, but responses to challenging questions revealed caution, especially around future growth and guidance: "We're just in an assessment, but I've been 1 month into the role and really just driving value is what we're after."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone has become less optimistic, with management more defensive, emphasizing ongoing assessment and a lack of full-year guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The CEO transition from Dan Burton to Benjamin Albert is a major change versus Q3 2025, with a stronger emphasis on leadership restructuring and operational rigor.
  • Unlike the previous quarter, the company is not providing annual guidance, citing ongoing strategic and operational review. In Q3, the company reaffirmed full-year guidance and offered early commentary for 2026, while now it only issues Q1 2026 figures.
  • The focus on simplifying metrics, reporting, and commercial narrative is more pronounced this quarter, along with a detailed review of product portfolio and cost structure.
  • Analyst questions have shifted from implementation and migration timelines to more pressing concerns about churn, ARR at risk, and the possibility of a sale or major strategic change.
  • The management tone has shifted from measured optimism and focus on execution to a more cautious and evaluative stance, deferring specific long-term commitments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Significant migration-related revenue headwinds, with $12.5 million in ARR identified as downsell and churn and $52 million subject to negotiation through 2027.
  • Margin pressure due to duplicate hosting and migration personnel costs, expected to persist into 2026 and partially into 2027.
  • Impairment charges of $110.2 million, primarily from market cap decrease and forecast revisions, affecting reported losses.
  • Professional services revenue continues to decline due to exit from less profitable contracts, and migration creates further near-term volatility.
  • The company is not providing full-year guidance, increasing uncertainty for investors.

Final Takeaway

Health Catalyst is undergoing a period of significant transformation following a CEO transition, with a renewed focus on operational discipline, leadership restructuring, and a comprehensive review of its business and product portfolio. The absence of full-year 2026 guidance and the acknowledgment of ongoing revenue headwinds from platform migrations mark a more cautious approach as the company seeks to simplify operations and reporting, prioritize higher-margin technology bookings, and address areas of churn and margin pressure. Investors are being asked for patience as the strategic review continues and clearer direction is expected by the next earnings call.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: TerrAscend outlines plans to expand New Jersey dispensary footprint to 10 while strengthening Pennsylvania capacity
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TerrAscend Corp. (TSND:CA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Jason Wild, Executive Chairman, reported that "in the fourth quarter, we generated $66.1 million in revenue, bringing full year 2025 revenue to approximately $261 million with gross margins for the quarter and full year of over 52%." He highlighted that TerrAscend achieved $16.7 million in adjusted EBITDA from continuing operations in Q4, and $67.8 million for the full year. Wild noted, "This marks TerrAscend 14th consecutive quarter of positive operating cash flow and tenth consecutive quarter of positive free cash flow." He also emphasized operational discipline, successful execution in New Jersey, Maryland, and Pennsylvania, and the completion of the Union Chill transaction, expanding the retail footprint to four dispensaries in New Jersey. Wild stated, "We completed a $79 million non-dilutive debt financing...the vast majority of our debt maturities are now extended to the second half of 2028, significantly eliminating near-term refinancing risk." He further described ongoing M&A evaluation, particularly in New Jersey and Pennsylvania, with a focus on disciplined, opportunistic acquisitions. Wild addressed regulatory developments, noting the federal executive order to expedite marijuana rescheduling, but stressed these reforms are not yet factored into company planning. He concluded, "we believe that our equity is significantly undervalued. With our normal course issuer bid in place, we will continue to execute on the buyback in a disciplined manner, while balancing growth investments, capital expenditures and selective M&A."

  • Ziad Ghanem, President & CEO, detailed state market performance: retail revenue in New Jersey increased, although wholesale declined due to price compression. Ghanem said, "With the addition of Union Chill at year-end, we believe we have entered 2026 as the highest grossing retailer in the state." He outlined successful brand performance, particularly growth in Legend vape and pre-roll sales. In Maryland, Ghanem reported an annual run rate of $75 million and gross margins nearing 60%, with retail revenue declining but wholesale increasing. In Pennsylvania, net revenue rose over 8% sequentially, driven by both retail and wholesale gains. Ghanem highlighted ongoing cultivation expansion in Pennsylvania, stating, "we reactivated 6 additional rooms in mid-December. This expansion will increase total flower output by roughly 50%." He also reported progress on the Michigan exit, with proceeds used to reduce debt. Ghanem closed by expressing strong confidence in TerrAscend’s future, citing "strong business fundamentals, a targeted M&A strategy, no material debt maturing until late 2028, consistent positive operating and free cash flow...and a strong leadership team."

  • Alisa Campbell, Senior Vice President of Corporate Finance & Accounting, stated, "Net revenue for the full year 2025 totaled $260.6 million compared to $268.1 million for 2024." She reported quarterly net revenue of $66.1 million, a 1.6% sequential increase. Campbell added, "Gross margin for the full year 2025 was 52.3%...Gross margin for the fourth quarter was 52.1%, remained stable quarter-over-quarter." She noted G&A expenses for the full year of $86.2 million, and a Q4 net loss from continuing operations of $0.5 million, reflecting improvement from the prior quarter. Campbell highlighted, "Cash and cash equivalents were $37.4 million as of December 31, 2025," and that Q4 marked the 14th consecutive quarter of positive operating cash flow.

Outlook

  • Campbell indicated that for Q1, "we expect revenue and gross margins to be similar to the results we reported for Q4." Ghanem reiterated the objective to expand the New Jersey retail footprint up to the maximum of 10 dispensaries, and emphasized ongoing evaluation of distressed M&A assets mainly in New Jersey and Pennsylvania.

Financial Results

  • Net revenue for Q4 was $66.1 million, up from $65.1 million in Q3. Gross margin for Q4 was 52.1%. Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $16.7 million, compared to $17 million in Q3. The net loss from continuing operations for Q4 was $0.5 million, a significant improvement from $9.9 million in Q3. Cash and equivalents at year-end were $37.4 million, up from $36.6 million at the end of Q3. The company reported $8.3 million in operating cash flow in Q4 and $6.6 million in free cash flow. CapEx for Q4 was $1.7 million, primarily for cultivation expansion.

Q&A

  • Frederico Yokota Gomes, ATB Cormark Capital Markets Inc.: "What is it that you're doing in [Pennsylvania] that's specialists and is driving that performance?...does that capacity expansion there, I guess, implies that maybe you're close to executing on M&A in the state...?" Ziad Ghanem responded, "We're very excited about Pennsylvania...We continue to improve on our quality. We continue to innovate and launch new products...We are expanding our cultivation in the state in preparation for all 3 things: the innovation, the new partnership, the trend in the state and also the potential for adult use...As far as M&A, this is not related to any M&A preparation even though we continue to look at accretive opportunity to go deeper in the state, but this is independent of any M&A opportunity."

  • Frederico Yokota Gomes, ATB Cormark Capital Markets Inc.: "how the pipeline looks in Ohio right now?...are you seeing good opportunities there?" Ghanem said, "We have close conversation in Ohio. We've had conversations. We didn't like the price in some cases...So learning from other states, the pricing did not make sense to us...this is not a reflection of a decrease in interest, more of the right price at the right time."

  • Kenric Tyghe, Canaccord Genuity: "is it fair to characterize it as waiting on the honeymoon pricing phase to end and that you're not going to be tempted to fit up a market that is clearly still very frothy...?" Ghanem replied, "Yes, the honeymoon has not been super full of honey as well...we are waiting for this to unfold, we are waiting for the licenses and the expansion to be better understood...we'll continue to wait on those."

  • Kenric Tyghe, Canaccord Genuity: "Could you speak to competitive dynamics? And is what we're seeing in New Jersey necessary rebalancing or normalization before that next leg of growth?..." Ghanem said, "Pricing pressure in New Jersey exist, but there's a way to combat it. And the way we have combated it so far and maintain our gross margin in the high 50s is by maintaining the quality by the innovation by launching new products...we're expanding cultivation in New Jersey to account for the new store that is coming with the Union Chill."

  • Kenric Tyghe, Canaccord Genuity: "is that a fair characterization that you gained share in both New Jersey and Pennsylvania in quarter?" Ghanem responded, "We did gain share in Pennsylvania. We did gain share in Maryland. We did not gain share in New Jersey...we held back and strategically changed our wholesale mix...when it comes wholesale finished good...we saw that increase, but it was balanced by the pricing pressure."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the sustainability of Pennsylvania growth, pricing dynamics in Ohio, and challenges in New Jersey’s competitive landscape. Their tone was neutral to slightly positive, with probing questions about market share, M&A discipline, and pricing power, but no overt skepticism or negativity.

  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout both prepared remarks and Q&A. Ghanem expressed high confidence in future performance, using phrases like "I am more confident in our future than I have ever been." Management was direct and transparent about challenges (e.g., New Jersey price compression), emphasizing disciplined decision-making and operational resilience.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone from both analysts and management remained relatively consistent, with management continuing to project confidence, and analysts maintaining a constructive but inquisitive approach.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remains stable, with management reiterating expectations for similar revenue and margins in Q1 2026. Strategic focus has intensified on expanding retail presence in New Jersey and scaling capacity in Pennsylvania, as well as seeking disciplined M&A in core markets. Analysts' focus shifted slightly more toward market share gains and pricing dynamics across key states. Key metric changes include improvement in net loss and stability in adjusted EBITDA margin, with continued positive cash flow. Management's confidence and positive sentiment were consistent with the previous quarter, while analysts’ tone remained neutral and focused on execution and strategic discipline.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing price compression in New Jersey and increased competition, but emphasized their response through innovation and quality. The company cited exposure to regulatory timelines for reforms and M&A pricing discipline, especially in Ohio where asset prices remain high. The exit from Michigan is nearly complete, with proceeds earmarked for debt reduction. Analysts’ concerns centered on competitive dynamics, pricing power, and the pace and pricing of M&A transactions.

Final Takeaway

TerrAscend’s management conveyed strong momentum exiting 2025, driven by operational discipline, strategic expansion in New Jersey and Pennsylvania, sustained positive cash flow, and a robust balance sheet. With no major debt maturities until late 2028, active share repurchases, and a focus on disciplined M&A, the company positions itself for continued growth and market leadership, while maintaining confidence in its ability to navigate pricing and competitive pressures in core markets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: uniQure upgraded at Mizuho on improved outlook for Huntington's asset AMT-130
data.description:
• Mizuho upgraded uniQure (QURE) to outperform from neutral saying the regulatory outlook for Huntington's disease asset AMT-130 is much improved following the upcoming departure of FDA official Vinay Prasad. 
  • The Japanese financial firm raised its price target to $35 from $12 (~107% upside based on Thursday's close).
  • Analyst Uy Ear said that Prasad's exit from the agency at the end of April "appears to signal an administration-level desire to restore greater regulatory flexibility in rare disease development," adding, "we believe this leadership change is constructive for uniQure, potentially reopening a path to a BLA based on the existing Phase 1/2 data or a Phase 3 trial leveraging natural history controls."
  • He also boosted his probability of success for AMT-130 to 55% from 15%, and advanced a potential launch to 2028 from 2030.

data.title: DoE plans $1.9B program for U.S. power grid upgrades
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Energy said Thursday it is launching a $1.9B funding effort aimed at accelerating upgrades to the nation's power grid needed to meet rising electricity demand and resource adequacy needs while lowering electricity costs.

The DoE said projects it selects through the Speed to Power through Accelerated Reconductoring and other Key Advanced Transmission Technology Upgrades, or SPARK, will need to show ​how reconductoring - replacing existing power lines with higher‑capacity conductors ​- combined with other advanced transmission ​technologies, can expand grid capacity, increase operational efficiency, lower prices for consumers, and improve overall system reliability and security of the U.S. electric grid.

Permitting problems and land disputes can cause building entirely new transmission lines to take a decade, while reconductoring replaces the wires already strung across existing towers with higher-capacity conductors that can carry more power.

The SPARK funding builds on the DoE's existing Grid Resilience and Innovation Partnerships program, which authorized up to $10.5B over five years to strengthen transmission infrastructure.

ETFs: (XLU), (DTCR), (IDGT), (TRFK), (VOLT)

data.title: Zedge outlines disciplined innovation and monetization strategy while expanding AI data offerings
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zedge, Inc. (ZDGE) Q2 2026

Management View

  • CEO Jonathan Reich emphasized that "the quality of our monetization continues to improve, and this is leading to record results." He noted Zedge achieved record levels of revenue and average revenue per monthly active user, attributing gains to "continued advertising optimization, record active subscription numbers and record Zedge Premium GTV." Reich also highlighted a focus on acquiring higher-value users and driving resilience in the marketplace despite continued contraction in monthly active users.
  • Reich discussed early progress in Data Seeds, describing the market as having an "incredible growth potential" and stating, "The appetite for AI training data is virtually insatiable, and we are productizing offerings, we believe can meet the needs of model builders and doing so intelligently and cost effectively." He said the company is "building an off-the-shelf or OTS catalog to drive down cost and accelerate order delivery," while noting revenue in the segment remains lumpy.
  • Reich identified that "our innovation team is humming," with two new alpha products launched this quarter, aiming for up to six for the fiscal year. He said, "Syncat, our first release under the product innovation team framework did not deliver the KPIs we were shooting for and we are ceasing development of this product."
  • Reich indicated that GuruShots "appears to be stabilizing and is being operated conservatively following last year's restructuring," and that cash strengthened to $19.1 million with no debt. He added, "we are now paying a quarterly dividend while continuing to invest in innovation and repurchasing shares when the market conditions are right."
  • CFO Yi Tsai reported, "Total revenue for the second quarter was $8.3 million, up 18.3% from last year." He highlighted that "Zedge Marketplace revenue was up over 21% year-over-year driven by strong advertising CPMs and subscription revenue." Tsai also noted, "Zedge+ subscription revenue increased 33% year-over-year, and our net active subscriber base grew 49%, reaching nearly 1.2 million subscribers."

Outlook

  • Management expects Data Seeds to "increase its contribution in the second half of fiscal 2026." Tsai also cautioned that last year included a one-time benefit to revenue of $450,000 related to an integration bonus from an ad partner that will not repeat in Q3 2026. Reich stated the company will continue to "strengthen monetization in the marketplace, build Data Seeds deliberately and expand our innovation pipeline in a disciplined way."

Financial Results

  • Tsai reported "deferred revenue, which primarily represents subscription-related revenue reached $6 million, up 5% sequentially and 39% year-over-year." He noted, "Zedge Premium GTV was up 15.7% from the year ago quarter and ARPMAU increased 47.6%."
  • Cost of revenue was "6.8% of revenue, which was up from 6.4% last year due to the reduction in partner discounts from Google Cloud Services as well as the introduction of Tapedeck licensing fees and Data Seeds production costs."
  • SG&A decreased about 6% to $6.7 million, reflecting restructuring savings offset by investment in Data Seeds and Tapedeck. GAAP loss from operations was $2.9 million compared to a loss of $2.2 million last year. The quarter included a $3.7 million asset impairment charge related to Emojipedia. GAAP net loss and loss per share were $2.3 million and $0.18, respectively. Non-GAAP net income was $0.8 million and EPS was $0.06. Adjusted EBITDA was $1.1 million, compared to negative $0.1 million last year.
  • Cash flow from operations was $0.9 million and free cash flow was $0.8 million for the quarter. The company ended the quarter with $19.1 million in cash and cash equivalents and no debt.

Q&A

  • Allen Klee, Maxim Group: Asked about the Data Seeds business model, margin expectations, and pipeline size. Reich replied, "the margins have been attractive... margins are also dependent on what type of deal it is, off-the-shelf will command a lower margin than custom made by and large, and depending on whether or not there is a middle man, meaning a marketplace that's evolved, that also impacts margin."
  • Klee: Inquired about expansion into data types beyond photos. Reich explained that Zedge is "focusing on multimodal data that can be images, audio and/or video," noting recent completion of a proof of concept on video and ongoing talks regarding audio.
  • Klee: Requested details on new alpha product launches. Reich said, "the 2 new ones literally are fresh out of the starting gate" and emphasized a modular, data-driven approach to product selection and rapid development.
  • Klee: Questioned subscription revenue momentum. Reich attributed ongoing growth to "our ongoing investment in optimizing our subscription offering and trying to find those pockets of prospective subscribers that will be attracted to what we had to offer."
  • Klee: Asked about Zedge Marketplace drivers. Reich pointed to the overlap of Q2 with year-end advertising spend and "a lot of work on the data science side in order to better segment users and optimize their performance from a monetization perspective."
  • Klee: Probed user acquisition strategy. Reich said, "marketing, find new marketing mechanisms... influencer market," new product features to drive daily engagement, and data science projects to "better isolate prospective new users."
  • Klee: Inquired about Tapedeck progress. Reich mentioned positive KPIs and a strategic shift toward expanding the music catalog rather than ongoing product development.
  • Klee: Asked about the importance of discovery for Tapedeck. Reich described in-app artist discovery features and direct artist partnerships to drive user engagement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on execution details and strategic priorities, with questions often seeking clarification on growth drivers, margins, and product innovation rather than expressing overt skepticism or concern.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks and remained measured but upbeat throughout the Q&A. Reich repeatedly emphasized discipline, operational focus, and a data-driven approach. Phrases such as "we believe that balance positions us well for the remainder of fiscal 2026" and "we are still in the midst of refining that process" demonstrate both confidence and ongoing evaluation.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone shifted from more frequent questions about operational risks and visibility to a greater focus on scaling opportunities and execution, while management's confidence remained steady, with more explicit references to infrastructure building and selective investment.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from emphasizing a "robust pipeline" and early Data Seeds traction to a more deliberate approach to scaling Data Seeds, with explicit mention of building a high-quality outbound pipeline and operational infrastructure.
  • Strategic focus sharpened on disciplined innovation, with the discontinuation of Syncat illustrating a willingness to cut underperforming products quickly. There is increased attention to monetization quality and higher-value user acquisition.
  • Analysts shifted focus from near-term risks (e.g., restructuring, segment losses) to detailed questions about product pipeline, margin structure, and growth levers in Data Seeds and Tapedeck.
  • Key metrics such as revenue growth, subscription growth, and ARPMAU saw notable year-over-year and sequential gains, while cost discipline continued through restructuring savings and SG&A reductions.
  • Management maintained a steady, confident tone, reinforcing disciplined capital allocation and operational rigor as the foundation of ongoing growth initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted "structural headwinds tied to the evolving field of search" for Emojipedia, resulting in a $3.7 million noncash impairment charge.
  • Revenue from Data Seeds remains "lumpy at this stage," with the challenge being "making the right bets, continuing to grow our library of relevant content and executing well in a rapidly developing market."
  • Tsai cautioned that last year's one-time $450,000 integration bonus from an ad partner will not recur in Q3 2026.
  • The company is being selective in Data Seeds opportunities, focusing only on feasible deals to avoid overextension and protect profitability.

Final Takeaway

Zedge management highlighted record monetization results and continued progress in subscription and marketplace revenue, while maintaining strong discipline in innovation and operational investment. The company is building infrastructure to scale its AI data business, making selective bets and emphasizing quality over volume in both user acquisition and product development. Strategic shifts include the discontinuation of underperforming initiatives and a heightened focus on cost control, while capital returns and innovation investments remain priorities. Management's tone signals a balanced approach to growth and profitability for the remainder of fiscal 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
